chat.text: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/draft-newbold-at-architecture/

chat.author: Bryan Newbold
chat.text: the Architecture doc was primarily intended for the BoF process, and isn't part of the current charter or deliverable. I'm happy to bump it back to active, but I don't think we intend it to be formally published

chat.author: Bumblefudge
chat.text: +1 to tagging them, even if they never get an RFC#, still good for the architecture doc to show up when someone checks the "home page" of the WG in datatracker :D

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: Drafts can show up in a WG's datatracker page with a tag that isn't yet adopted.

chat.author: Emelia Smith
chat.text: @justin maybe there's a need for you and the chairs to sync on working group tooling since you know it really well?

chat.author: Tim Trautmann
chat.text: I tried both Chrome and Safari. No sound. Oh well.

chat.author: Emelia Smith
chat.text: I think we'll essentially have a point at which we are working on a drafts, and then a call for adoption of the draft — importantly a draft does not need to resolve every issue, and the call for adoption will raise additional issues

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text:
For chairs, you can go to this  link and manually add the missing draft:
https://datatracker.ietf.org/group/atp/documents/

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: They did it. The confusion was that the chairs were asking for new drafts to have the atp tag so they would show up automagically.

chat.author: Paul Rohr
chat.text: For any other newbies who aren't getting sound, I had to leave the room + reenter from the main agenda from the day.  That kicked off a process of getting my microphone + video connected.  After that, I got both sound + the ability to remotely participate

chat.author: Bumblefudge
chat.text: thanks paul! good to see you

chat.author: Emelia Smith
chat.text:
in the frontmatter on the draft markdown is that you need to add the group/venue info in, for example this automatically appeared in the OAuth WG tracker: https://github.com/ThisIsMissEm/draft-oauth-authorization-management-uri/blob/main/draft-emelia-oauth-authorization-management-uri.md?plain=1#L33-L41
The existing markdown documents that are I-D's don't have that frontmatter in their current version, as they predate the WG. So just updating that metadata allows them to stay as a draft but also automatically appear in datatracker

chat.author: Emelia Smith
chat.text: So it's just a matter for daniel / bryan to publish a new I-D version which includes that group metadata

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: If it requires interoperability, then it belongs in the RFC.  That may mean setting up a separate RFC talking about cryptographic agility.

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: @ted +1

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: IPMC is the current IETF

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: IPR holder

chat.author: Eli Mallon
chat.text: I'm not to worried about the governance of this one part of a URL path

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: These are maybe just strings, that deployments need to agree on for interop, but where other networks could make different choices?

chat.author: Emelia Smith
chat.text: I think even with resolving lexicons, you could have a specification backing the lexicon

chat.author: Emelia Smith
chat.text: and then the lexicon as an entirety says via a description comment "this lexicon is specified in this document: <url>"

chat.author: Phil Schleihauf
chat.text: operational considerations would be really helpful for the http api endpoints, as a sync library implementer -- right now it wasn't really clear  if i should make them configurable, or hard-code for the network i'm targeting, etc

chat.author: Bumblefudge
chat.text: +1 Orie, minimal definition of API/string shape might be enough

chat.author: Eli Mallon
chat.text: Oh interesting, I'll be talking about relay role in my presentation a bit - for the atmoq use case it matters whether relays require negotiation/multiple formats

chat.author: Eli Mallon
chat.text: As opposed to like one canonical format that I could broadcast unidirectionally

chat.author: Bumblefudge
chat.text: negotiation of content, you say

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: Agree with identifying the privacy considerations for each draft individually. Referencing the privacy considerations of other drafts is something other authors of protocol suite docs have done when needed.

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: Endpoints should be .well-known when we get there...

chat.author: Andrew Newton
chat.text: @phil can you make that comment at the mic?

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: Reminder to those in the queue we have 5 minutes before we move on in the agenda.

chat.author: Ted Hardie
chat.text: Obviously if you have a full-on privacy BCP that's great and no need to fold it back into an architecture doc.

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: There will be backwards incompatibility when we get to PQC...

chat.author: Michael Richardson
chat.text: I advise against having more than I-D per git repo.  It's not a big deal, but I think your life will ultimately be easier if you ever have to bisect etc...

chat.text: :clap:

include blobs in these docs

chat.text: Ed25519 in webcrypto now, so maybe that's an argument for it being worth some amount of hassle?

chat.author: Michael Richardson
chat.text: I concur... the next crypto change should be towards quantum-safe....

chat.author: Eric Rescorla
chat.text: yeah ML-DSA is non-malleable

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: great

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: Should also make sure the protocol does not depend on non-malleability

chat.author: Eric Rescorla
chat.text: Apparently it already has malleability problems

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: seems like its not worth doing anything other than adding support for MLDSA

chat.author: Ryan Barrett
chat.text: yeah low-S mitigation :oh_no:

chat.author: Eric Rescorla
chat.text: Well ML-DSA isn't EC, so it's not a curve really

chat.author: Justin Richer
chat.text: "there are two curves inside you..."

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: @Orie, killin Secp256k1 is worth doing.

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: NIST P-256 is pretty solidly specified.

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: I disagree... its already going to be in lots of data we gotta keep around for ever... adding another pre quantum alg to a protocol now... seems not worth it.

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: Fair. s/curve/signature alg/

chat.author: Bryan Newbold
chat.text: should note that different from TLS, there is no opportunity for negotiation here: signed commits get blasted out on the firehose, and consumers can either verify them or not

chat.author: Stuart Card
chat.text: secp256k1 is nice for Bitcoin interop. Ed25519 is nice for interop with lots of things inc. DRIP.

chat.author: Ryan Barrett
chat.text: yeah no negotiation is a key point

chat.author: Bryan Newbold
chat.text: social negotiation (talking to all the operators) has been the path so far!

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: Why migrate twice... you will need to migrate to a PQ signature eventually.

chat.author: Stuart Card
chat.text: I don't know how to live w/o crypto agility.

chat.author: Maxine Fritz
chat.text: i would like to see if anyone can produce numbers on the amount of active secp256k1 keys on the network

chat.author: Eli Mallon
chat.text: +1 Orie, it's gonna be a PITA so let's not do it twice

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: If people are worried about Quantum Computing, if any elliptic curve in use is broken, Secp256k1 will be the first to fall.

chat.author: Mallory Knodel
chat.text: The queue is closed because we have 6 minutes for the rest of Daniel's slides.

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: DNSSEC was not on my bingo card for today

chat.author: Stuart Card
chat.text: +1 phillip

chat.author: Bryan Newbold
chat.text:
IMHO adding a post-quantum option is the top priority.
I'm open to removing secp256k1 in theory, but would not prioritize the effort needed over adding MLDSA (or whatever)

chat.author: Orie Steele
chat.text: agree with that @PHB... biggest pay off : )

chat.author: Michael Richardson
chat.text:
Orie Steele said:

agree with that @PHB... biggest pay off : )

chat.author: David Schinazi
chat.text: +1 Bryan, yeah removing is not a priority. I might just tell new implementations/deployments not to use it

chat.author: Phil Schleihauf
chat.text: oh periodic cursor info would be really nice

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: We need to think through what 'removing' means. Basically, it means tell people to stop signing stuff with that curve.

chat.author: Ryan Barrett
chat.text: cursor info :+1:

chat.author: Phil Schleihauf
chat.text: cursor info: it's currently hard to balance fast dead-connection detection (relay subscription) from just a slow upstream (pds-direct subscription). seems like this also solves that.

chat.author: Phillip Hallam-Baker
chat.text: On PQC, we will never use MLDSA to sign individual firehose increments, it would blow it up. We are surely going to be going into signatures over trees or something of the sort to shrink the overhead. So... cant do it now.

chat.author: Maxine Fritz
chat.text: phillip: as well as telling people to stop verifying stuff that indicates secp256k1 is in use

chat.author: Peter Van Garderen

chat.text: w.r.t. applications verifying the JSON presentation against the actual CBOR; is there room to specify that applications should, in the presence of an ambiguous numeric type in the JSON presentation, try both encodings (as an int vs as a float) and treat "one works, but not the other" as valid?

chat.author: bengo
chat.text: We need to adopt a repo doc so we can make design decisions on that are normative based on evaluating multiple options not just descriptive of past non-WG decisions. eg DASL/dag-cbor/DRISL is weird and has its own whole CID / “multicodec” registry of types at no particular SDO that is essentially duplicative with IETF media type or CBOR tag registries. Personally I think it makes more sense to define an MST with “normal” dCBOR not these esoteric things
